hook: On his first day cleaning an empty old house, Tom finds a video camera hidden in a dusty cupboard — and watches something he was never meant to see.
goal: Tom must clean the upstairs bedroom before Mrs. Cole returns to lock up, but his curiosity pulls him away from the job.

start_state.location: Upstairs bedroom of the abandoned house (loc_001)
items: house key given by Mrs. Cole
knowledge: The house has been empty for many years
Mrs. Cole hired Tom to clean the rooms for a new owner
Tom must finish and leave by 5 p.m. so Mrs. Cole can lock up

continuity_rules: This is the first episode — there is no prior scene to continue from
Must not reveal the family's name or identity — reserved for Episode 2
Must not let Tom lift the loose floorboard or retrieve the box
key_events: Tom sweeps the upstairs bedroom and pushes old furniture aside; when he opens a dusty cupboard to clean inside it, he finds an old video camera sitting on the shelf.
Tom's curiosity takes over — he checks the camera, finds it still has battery, and presses play; he watches the small screen closely and does not examine the outside of the camera case at all.

On the screen, a recording from the past: a man and a woman with a young boy stand in the same room; the man lifts a floorboard, places a metal box beneath it, and puts the board back; the family's faces look frightened; the tape ends without explanation.

Tom kneels on the bedroom floor and finds one board that looks different from the others — it is loose; he grips the edge and is about to pull it up when Mrs. Cole calls his name from downstairs; he leaves the camera on the windowsill and goes down, the board still closed beneath him.
major_reveal: A family once deliberately hid a metal box under the floorboards of this very room — and they looked frightened when they did it.
new_thread_created: Who is the family on the tape, what did they hide in the metal box, and why did they look so afraid?
tone_guardrails: Keep the atmosphere physical and concrete — dust, creaking boards, dim light, old furniture
The family on the tape should appear frightened but not violent — restrained dread, not drama
Mrs. Cole's arrival must feel natural and routine — she is simply doing her job, not acting suspiciously
Show Tom handling the camera without examining its outer case — he looks only at the screen; this plants the unseen label for Episode 2 without cheating
forbidden_moves: Do not reveal the name 'Hartley' or any identity for the family — reserved for Episode 2
Do not let Tom lift the loose floorboard or see what is beneath it
Do not make Mrs. Cole behave suspiciously or threateningly — she is neutral and businesslike
Do not have Tom notice any writing or label on the camera case
ending_hook: Tom goes home knowing there is something hidden beneath that loose board — but what is inside the box, and why did the family on the tape look so afraid?
